mentions.text: @EccShibaInuInv Idk enough about lithium batteries to be comparing $DFLI vs $QS

@Yeah_Dave I don't do your hold for dear life strategy with a few individual stocks and hedge along the way. 

I personally hedge by shifting portfolio concentration by doing sector rotation. 

For example Iran/US tensions $CVX / $XLE and likely defense goes up, trim other weightings. These are natural hedges rather than puts, and as long as more things go green then red, it's effective. 

Post-greenland peace deal for example -> trim $OSS and related sector correlation to military. 

Post Google capex -> up exposure to semi trade/photonics BOM. 

I think you focus more on small cap like $TE, $QS where my portfolio is split between semi supply chain bottlenecks + energy.  

Strategy would probably be a lot different, mine is extremely active management based on macro catalysts, supply chain mapping, and earning forecasts.
